message:
feat(writing/slack-mattermost): rewrite intro in authentic voice, add hero illustration + skill-catalog + release-notes + phase-enumeration callouts, de-slopify the whole article

This is one combined commit because the pieces are entangled in the same
file and it is genuinely the same editorial pass.

------------------------------------------------------------
 Hero illustration  ── assets/slack_migration_post_illustration.webp (NEW)
------------------------------------------------------------

New cwebp-q92 WebP of the Slack → Mattermost illustration (1402×1122,
~348 KB). Imported with `next/image` and placed in the hero between the
subtitle and stat chips, wrapped in a cyan/purple radial-gradient glow
(blur 40px, scale 1.2) and a soft rounded border, mirroring the pattern
used for the Overprompting article's hero illustration. `priority` +
`placeholder="blur"` so the LCP tile shows immediately on first paint
without layout shift.

------------------------------------------------------------
 Intro rewrite  ── ruthless de-slopify of the lead paragraph
------------------------------------------------------------

Killed the AI-slop lead ("There is a tweet from April 2026 that I keep
coming back to...") which was doubly bad: the false-temporal ("I keep
coming back to") when the tweet is from this week, and the performative
literary framing in general.

Replaced with a direct setup that names the pattern ("Every few months
now, some founder posts a screenshot of Slack's latest renewal quote
and it goes viral..."), links out to the actual tweet
(x.com/anothercohen/status/2044255819290525739), and lands the numbers
the reader cares about (21k quote, 6k prior bill, 3.5x).

Also added a new 6th intro paragraph that sets up the broader framing
the author introduced on X: this isn't really about chat software, it
is about expensive enterprise SaaS whose moat is mostly migration pain
(Jira, Splunk, etc.). Mirrors the "A pattern, not a migration" closing
section and gives the piece a better through-line.

------------------------------------------------------------
 §1.2 skill-catalog callout (direct links + login caveat)
------------------------------------------------------------

Three jeffreys-skills.md catalog URLs, each annotated with its role
(Phase 1 Slack-side extraction, Phase 2 Mattermost-side deploy/import,
Phase 3 ongoing maintenance). An amber "Heads up" note makes it
explicit that the URLs 404 unless the reader is signed in to a
jeffreys-skills.md subscription, and ties that sign-in to the same
account the `jsm` CLI authenticates against below, so the reader who
resolves the 404 first has already done the auth step the install
section otherwise surprises them with.

------------------------------------------------------------
 §1.2 "What shipped in the latest release · April 2026" callout
------------------------------------------------------------

Compact release-notes block covering v2 of Phase 1, v2 of Phase 2,
v3 of Phase 3, each with a short paragraph of standout bullets drawn
from the catalog's own announcement copy. Purpose: a reader landing
on the article with a cached mental model from a previous version
immediately sees what is new.

------------------------------------------------------------
 Content enumerations the article was missing
------------------------------------------------------------

- §3.8 "What Phase 1 actually ships: 21 scripts, 6 subagents, 4
  validators": two-column card. Left column names the four
  non-negotiable validators (artifact hash + layout, JSONL ordering +
  linkage, enrichment completeness, raw-vs-enriched-vs-JSONL count
  reconciliation). Right column names the six focused subagents
  (acquisition-auditor, gap-hunter, token-exposure-redteam,
  compliance-approval-auditor, slack-plan-tier-router,
  reconciliation-analyst) with a one-line purpose each, and notes
  that each subagent emits a verdict of ready | blocked | needs-review.
  Closes with the four-file Phase 1 → Phase 2 handoff contract
  (handoff.json, verification.md, unresolved-gaps.md, evidence-pack.json).

- §4.9 "seven Go/No-Go gates, not just the ready gate": zoom-out table
  inside the existing ready-gate section naming every gate the Phase 2
  pipeline walks through in order (intake-valid, infra-provisioned,
  stack-deployed, import-reconciled, staging-passed, war-room-GO,
  cutover-complete), with the written-down pass criterion for each.

- §12.2 "The eight maintain.sh stages": new subsection at the top of
  Part 12 enumerating every Phase 3 stage (health, update-os,
  update-mattermost, db-backup, restore-drill, rotate-credentials,
  incident, disaster-recovery) with cadence and what-it-does columns.
  Highlights the 90-day restore-drill gate on update-mattermost.

------------------------------------------------------------
 De-slopify pass on the ~3500 other lines of prose
------------------------------------------------------------

~50 targeted edits killing the usual AI tells:

- em-dashes used as sentence connectives, recast to commas,
  semicolons, colons, parens, or sentence splits (em-dash count in
  the article file went from 145 to 39; the remaining 39 are all in
  JSX comments, TOC labels like `Part 1 — Workstation setup`, or
  shell comments inside `<Code>` blocks, none of them in prose)
- "There is a X that I keep coming back to" and variants, killed
- "It is not X, it is Y" / "Not A, but B" formula, recast
- "It's worth noting," "Here's why," "Here's the thing," deleted
- hedge vocabulary (`genuinely`, `simply`, `truly`, `quite`,
  `fundamentally`, `essentially` used as filler), deleted
- "Interestingly," / "Notably," / "Remarkably," openers, deleted
- meta "In this article, we..." phrasing, rewritten as direct prose

Voice target was the author's own Grassmann essay
(content/writing/hermann_grassmann_nature_of_abstractions.md):
conversational, first-person where natural, opinionated asides,
longer flowing sentences with parentheticals, discourse markers
("Basically," / "Well," / "Anyway,"), no performative setups.

------------------------------------------------------------
 Verified
------------------------------------------------------------

- bunx eslint components/slack-migration-article.tsx: clean
- bun run build: exit 0, /writing/slack-mattermost-migration still
  prerendered as static (○) with the illustration picked up by the
  manifest.
